content.text:
Now I have a clear view of atom C:0. From this image, I can definitively identify the neighbor atoms directly bonded to atom 0 (C):

- H:67 (hydrogen)
- H:65 (hydrogen) 
- H:66 (hydrogen)
- C:1 (carbon)

Atom C:0 is a carbon atom with four covalent bonds: three to hydrogen atoms (H:67, H:65, H:66) and one to carbon atom C:1. This forms a methyl group (-CH₃) attached to the rest of the molecule through the C:1 atom.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file name "geomconf_738.xyz", the geomconf_index for this molecule is 738.

So the neighbor atoms of atom 0 (C) are:
- Atom index 67, element H
- Atom index 65, element H  
- Atom index 66, element H
- Atom index 1, element C

Geomconf_index: 738

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 0, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
